rows.label: Country
rows.html: Italy [1]

rows.label: State / region
rows.html: Trentino (Trentino-Alto Adige) [1]

rows.label: City / nearest town
rows.html: Canazei (also Campitello di Fassa, Alba) [1]

rows.label: Mountain area
rows.html: Dolomites · between Col Rodella and Belvedere (Sella / Marmolada group) [1]

rows.label: Drive from Berlin
rows.html: 871 km · 9:49 h ↗ Google Maps Directions API

rows.label: Lift type
rows.html: 6–7 modern facilities: gondolas, cable cars, a Des Alpes chairlift and the Alba–Col dei Rossi funifor; new Campitello 3S gondola from 13 June 2026. Capacity Not found [1] [4]

rows.label: Season
rows.html: 2026 lift openings staged from 30 May, most running by 20 June; park dates broadly mid-June to late September [4] [1]

rows.label: Operating hours
rows.html: Approx. 08:30–17:20 (varies by facility) [1]

rows.label: Gravity Card
rows.html: Yes — listed in the Gravity Card network [3]

rows.label: Character
rows.html: One of Europe's most established gravity/enduro destinations in the UNESCO Dolomites — raw alpine terrain, big elevation changes and iconic lines, an Enduro World Series / EDR World Cup venue. [2] [4]

rows.label: Trail types
rows.html: Natural technical enduro, fast flow tracks, downhill and North Shore elements, across three main slopes. [1]

rows.label: Difficulty spread
rows.html: 17 named trails: 7 blue, 4 red, 3 black (plus easier/connector lines) — 7 4 3 . Some sources cite 18 trails across the wider district [1] [2]

rows.label: Vertical drop
rows.html: Trails descend from ~2,000 m; up to ~800 m of vertical (Infinity trail) and 30 m (Rabbe trail) at the other end [1]

rows.label: Longest descent
rows.html: Infinity trail — 7.0 km with 800 m vertical [1]

rows.label: Family friendliness
rows.html: Flow blue trails suit progressing riders, but the district skews toward enduro/technical riding rather than young-family use [1]

rows.label: Beginner-friendliness
rows.html: Moderate — seven blue trails offer entry-level flow; freeride instruction available (group 3 h, €30 pp, min 5) [1]

rows.label: Skills area
rows.html: Not found — no dedicated beginner skills area or magic-carpet belt mentioned by the operator [1]

rows.label: Standout features
rows.html: The legendary Tutti Frutti trail (Proline, 2.5 km) and the long Infinity descent; new 3S gondola improves access to Col Rodella and the Sellaronda [5] [4]

rows.label: Bike shop / repair / wash
rows.html: Three rental shops (Northland, Detomas, Peak Sport); reviewers note a single bike-wash lot at Pecol (none at the bottom) [1] [2]

rows.label: Camper-van / camping
rows.html: Specific on-site camper/Stellplatz Not found; campsites exist elsewhere in Val di Fassa [2]

rows.label: Reputation / reviews
rows.html: Wanderlog aggregate 3.4/5 from 45 reviews — praised for difficulty labelling and fast lifts, criticised for trail signage and maintenance vs. the €38 day price [2]
